title: Miami’s Best Sushi Restaurants

whoInvolved: Eater Miami, a leading food and restaurant publication covering the Miami dining scene, has curated this list by evaluating restaurants across multiple sushi categories and price points throughout the city.
whyMatters: As Miami's food scene continues to gain national recognition, quality sushi establishments have become a key draw for both residents and visitors seeking authentic and innovative Japanese cuisine. This guide helps diners navigate an increasingly competitive market and make informed choices about where to spend their dining dollars.
moreContext: Miami's sushi landscape has evolved significantly over the past decade, with the city developing a sophisticated culinary culture that extends beyond its traditional Cuban and Latin American heritage. The proliferation of omakase restaurants and Nikkei (Japanese-Peruvian fusion) concepts reflects broader national food trends and Miami's growing appeal as a destination for upscale dining. The diversity of options—from quick lunch counters to investment-level omakase experiences—demonstrates the maturation of the local sushi market and increased consumer demand for varied price points and dining styles.

title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour is emerging as Miami Beach's premier lifestyle destination, offering residents and visitors a sophisticated alternative to the tourist-saturated Ocean Drive scene. The neighborhood is establishing itself as a wellness-focused, walkable community on the bay side of South Beach.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated is highlighting Sunset Harbour, a neighborhood in Miami Beach that caters to both locals and tourists seeking an upscale experience. The area's identity is shaped by local businesses, wellness establishments, and lifestyle-oriented merchants who prioritize community atmosphere over mass tourism.
whyMatters: This shift reflects broader Miami Beach trends toward neighborhood-specific branding and quality-of-life considerations rather than generic tourism. For residents and businesses, it positions Sunset Harbour as an attractive alternative for those seeking curated local experiences, potentially driving property values and attracting targeted demographics seeking authentic urban living.
moreContext: Miami Beach has historically been dominated by Ocean Drive's party-centric tourist culture, but neighborhoods like Sunset Harbour represent a maturing market segment prioritizing wellness, walkability, and local authenticity. Located on the quieter bay side rather than the beachfront, Sunset Harbour offers a more residential feel while maintaining proximity to South Beach's amenities. This trend aligns with post-pandemic consumer preferences favoring community-oriented destinations. The neighborhood's growth reflects Miami's broader evolution into a year-round destination for affluent residents seeking lifestyle quality alongside urban sophistication.

title: Taste the Gables is Back with 75 Restaurants
summary: The City of Coral Gables’ popular restaurant month, Taste the Gables, returns this July with its most expansive lineup to date. More than 75 restaurants, ranging from Michelin-recognized destinations to neighborhood favorites and varied cuisines, are participating, showcasing…
whatHappening: Taste the Gables, Coral Gables' signature restaurant month, is returning this July with 75 participating restaurants—the largest roster in the event's history. Diners will have access to a diverse selection spanning Michelin-recognized fine dining establishments, casual neighborhood spots, and varied international cuisines.
whoInvolved: The City of Coral Gables is organizing the event, positioning it as a marquee dining promotion for the upscale Miami enclave. Participating restaurants range from high-end culinary destinations to accessible local favorites across multiple cuisine categories.
whyMatters: The expanded lineup signals strong recovery and growth in Coral Gables' restaurant sector post-pandemic, attracting both locals and tourists during the typically slower summer month. For participating restaurants, the coordinated promotion drives foot traffic and customer acquisition during a traditionally slower season, while boosting the city's reputation as a dining destination.
moreContext: Taste the Gables has established itself as an annual marquee event for the Coral Gables dining scene, similar to other city-branded restaurant weeks in major markets. The inclusion of Michelin-recognized venues alongside neighborhood favorites reflects Coral Gables' positioning as a diverse dining hub rather than exclusively upscale. Restaurant months typically feature prix-fixe menus or special promotions to encourage sampling across establishments. The July timing capitalizes on summer tourism while helping restaurants combat seasonal revenue dips.

title: New Restaurant Reviews + Eating News – Part 2
summary: More new restaurant reviews, vinyl nights, Persian happy hour with delish light bites, restaurant closings and more in Part 2 of eating news in June. For Part 1, click here.
whatHappening: Miami's restaurant scene continues to evolve with new openings, themed dining experiences, and closures marking the end of June. The dining landscape features everything from vinyl listening nights to Persian cuisine happy hours with light appetizers.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated, a local dining and lifestyle publication, is documenting the changes across Miami's restaurant community. Various independent restaurants and dining establishments throughout Miami are participating in new concepts and closures.
whyMatters: Tracking restaurant openings and closures provides insight into Miami's dining trends and economic health within the hospitality sector. These changes reflect shifting consumer preferences toward experiential dining (vinyl nights, themed happy hours) and evolving cuisine popularity, particularly international cuisines like Persian.
moreContext: This is Part 2 of Miami Curated's June eating news roundup, indicating ongoing coverage of the restaurant industry throughout the month. The inclusion of both new concepts and closures shows the dynamic nature of Miami's food scene, where established venues exit while innovative dining experiences emerge. Persian cuisine happy hours suggest growing demand for Middle Eastern dining options in the market. The mention of vinyl nights reflects a broader hospitality trend of combining food service with entertainment experiences to create differentiated dining destinations.

title: New Restaurant Reviews + More Eating News: Part 1
summary: Reviews of new restaurant openings including Casa Tua Cucina Wynwood, "Meet me at Milos "spritz and aperitivo special, a Dubai chewy cookie, new top spot for coffee lovers with a Norwegian dessert, first Cuban restaurant to get a Michelin star and a bundle for non alcoholic…
whatHappening: Miami's dining scene is experiencing a wave of new restaurant openings and culinary innovations. Notable debuts include Casa Tua Cucina in Wynwood, a new coffee destination featuring Norwegian desserts, and Miami's first Michelin-starred Cuban restaurant, alongside specialty beverage concepts like aperitivo bars and non-alcoholic drink bundles.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated is covering multiple independent and established restaurants entering the Miami market. Key players include Casa Tua Cucina, a Wynwood-based establishment, and various specialty dining concepts ranging from coffee shops to Michelin-recognized Cuban cuisine operators.
whyMatters: These openings signal Miami's continued evolution as a serious culinary destination attracting diverse dining experiences and international culinary standards. The Michelin star recognition for a Cuban restaurant particularly validates Miami's cultural cuisine on a global stage, while specialty concepts like aperitivo bars and non-alcoholic options reflect changing consumer dining preferences and social trends.
moreContext: Miami's restaurant landscape has undergone significant transformation over the past decade, with the city increasingly attracting chef-driven concepts and international recognition. The emergence of Michelin-starred establishments in Miami reflects the guide's expanded coverage of American cities beyond traditional fine-dining capitals. Consumer interest in experiential dining—from aperitivo culture to specialty coffee and non-alcoholic beverages—reflects broader hospitality trends emphasizing social experiences and wellness-conscious options. Wynwood's designation as a dining destination hub demonstrates the neighborhood's evolution from arts district to comprehensive culinary attraction.

title: A Traditional Italian Fave Made Even Better: Il Mulino
summary: Il Mulino New York located at Acqualina Resort, the Five Star beachfront property which recently celebrated its 20th anniversary, has always been one of my favorite traditional Italian restaurants in Miami. It’s authentic, gives good quality/value, the food is reliably very…
whatHappening: Il Mulino New York, a traditional Italian restaurant at Acqualina Resort in Miami, continues to deliver authentic Italian cuisine with strong quality-to-value offerings. The establishment remains a standout dining destination in Miami's competitive restaurant scene.
whoInvolved: Il Mulino New York operates at Acqualina Resort, a five-star beachfront property that recently marked its 20th anniversary. The restaurant brings the acclaimed Il Mulino brand to Miami's luxury hospitality sector.
whyMatters: Established Italian restaurants that maintain consistency and authenticity in a market flooded with dining options help define a city's culinary reputation. For Acqualina Resort, having a reliable flagship restaurant enhances guest experience and drives repeat business and referrals.
moreContext: Acqualina Resort, a premier beachfront destination, recently celebrated two decades of operation in Miami. Il Mulino New York represents the property's commitment to high-end dining by partnering with an established Italian restaurant brand. Traditional Italian restaurants face pressure from trendy concepts, yet Il Mulino has maintained relevance by focusing on authentic preparation, quality ingredients, and consistent execution. The restaurant's presence at a luxury resort positions it as both a destination for hotel guests and local diners seeking reliable upscale Italian cuisine.

title: Miami’s New Modern Mexican Hotspot—What You Need to Know
summary: We went to the Mexican Miami less than three weeks after opening and it was buzzy and many of its seats taken with individuals at the bar, small groups at high top round tables, and others headed to the private rooms celebrating a special occasion. The word has gotten out…
whatHappening: A new modern Mexican restaurant has opened in Miami and is already generating significant buzz less than three weeks into operation. The venue is packed with diners at the bar, high-top tables, and private dining spaces hosting celebrations.
whoInvolved: Mexican Miami is the new establishment making waves in Miami's dining scene. The restaurant has quickly attracted attention from the local food community and diners seeking contemporary Mexican cuisine.
whyMatters: The rapid popularity signals strong demand for elevated Mexican dining experiences in Miami's competitive restaurant market. This early success could establish a new benchmark for modern Mexican concepts in the city and attract additional investment in the category.
moreContext: Miami has long been a hub for Latin American cuisine, but modern Mexican restaurants offering upscale experiences represent a growing segment. The venue's design emphasizes social dining with bar seating, communal high-tops, and private spaces—a layout that encourages both casual visits and special occasions. Word-of-mouth momentum in the first three weeks suggests the restaurant has resonated with Miami's dining audience, a critical indicator for new restaurant longevity in the market.
